raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Grüß Gott! Herzlich willkommen in meiner Bäckerei am Naschmarkt. Was darf’s denn heute sein?

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Ich möchte einen Roggenbrot. Ha-ha, haben Sie Roggenbrot?

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Ja, freilich haben wir Roggenbrot - ganz frisch heute! Möchten Sie ein ganzes oder lieber ein halbes?

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Sehr gern. Ein halbes Roggenbrot - das macht drei Euro fünfzig. Zahlen Sie bar oder mit Karte?

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Bitte sehr, stecken Sie die Karte hier rein. Und hier ist Ihr Roggenbrot, schön eingepackt. Danke und auf Wiedersehen!

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Gerne! Auf Wiedersehen und einen schönen Tag noch! -

reviewer_output.overall_summary: Robert completed a short but functional bakery transaction in German, demonstrating solid use of polite register and key phrases like 'Ich hätte gern'. Two clear errors appeared — a gender mistake on 'Roggenbrot' and a missing subject pronoun in his payment response — but overall communication was successful and natural.

errors.explanation: 'Brot' is neuter (das Brot), so the accusative indefinite article is 'ein', not 'einen' (which is masculine accusative).
German requires an explicit subject pronoun; dropping 'Ich' is only acceptable in very informal written contexts (e.g. texting) and sounds incomplete in spoken conversation.

vocabulary_highlights.note: used correctly — polite Konjunktiv II form, ideal for shop/restaurant requests
new this session — Austrian/Bavarian regional expression used by Frau Berger; worth recognising passively
new this session — past participle of 'einpacken', useful in shop contexts

vocabulary_highlights.english: Hello / Good day (lit. 'May God greet you')
vocabulary_highlights.note: new this session — standard Austrian/Southern German greeting; Robert should recognise and could use it
used correctly — appropriate and natural closing phrase

strengths: Used the polite Konjunktiv II form 'Ich hätte gern' correctly, showing good register awareness for formal transactions.
Self-corrected mid-sentence ('Ich möchte einen Roggenbrot. Ha-ha, haben Sie Roggenbrot?'), demonstrating real-time awareness of his own speech.
Maintained a polite, appropriate register throughout the entire interaction without slipping into overly casual language.
Responded naturally and concisely to Frau Berger's question about whole or half loaf, keeping the conversation flowing.
reviewer_output.next_focus: Practice the gender and accusative articles for common food and shop nouns (das Brot, die Semmel, der Kuchen, das Brötchen), with a focus on accusative 'ein/eine/einen' to avoid the 'einen Brot' type error.

scaffold_delivered: Meine Frau hätte gerne den Apfelstrudel.
Einen Moment bitte — ich überlege kurz.
